title: BIRD Energy and U.S.-Israel Energy Center Reauthorization Act of 2025
short_title: BIRD Energy and U.S.-Israel Energy Center Reauthorization Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill renews a partnership between the United States and Israel to work together on energy projects until 2035. The two countries will get more money to do research and run energy centers together. They will also start working on new types of energy like hydrogen and fusion power. The bill also includes working together to protect energy systems from computer attacks.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committees on Foreign Affairs, and Homeland Security, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the BIRD Energy and U.S.-Israel Energy Center Reauthorization Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings
sections.text: Congress finds these facts. The Binational Industrial Research and Development BIRD Energy Foundation started in two thousand nine to help the United States and Israel work together on energy development. BIRD Energy helps solve energy problems and find new opportunities that both countries care about. It focuses on making clean energy technologies that can be sold to customers. This improves how well both countries compete in business, creates jobs, and supports new companies with fresh ideas. The money for science research and development helps both countries get better at many things. These include protecting energy systems from computer attacks, renewable energy, storing energy, and fossil energy like oil and gas.

sections.title: Section 3 Extension and Reauthorization of Energy Cooperation Authorities
sections.text: This section changes two existing laws to give more money and extend the time for energy cooperation programs. For cooperative research, the law changes the amount from two million dollars to five million dollars. It also changes the time from just fiscal year two thousand twenty one to each fiscal year from two thousand twenty six through two thousand thirty one. For the Energy Center, the law changes the amount from four million dollars to seven million dollars. It also changes the time from just fiscal year two thousand twenty one to each fiscal year from two thousand twenty six through two thousand thirty one.

sections.title: Section 4 Additional United States-Israel Energy Cooperation Categories
sections.text: This section adds new types of energy work that the United States and Israel can cooperate on together. The new categories are hydrogen energy, fusion energy, industrial decarbonization (helping factories reduce carbon pollution), carbon management (controlling and storing carbon), agrivoltaics (combining farming with solar panels), grid modernization (updating the electricity system), and cybersecurity for energy infrastructure (protecting energy systems from computer attacks).

sections.title: Section 5 Reauthorization of United States-Israel Energy Cooperation
sections.text: This section extends the United States-Israel Energy Cooperation program. The program was set to end in two thousand twenty four, but this law extends it to two thousand thirty five.
